---
title: "UOM Suggestion Logic in the Recognition Pipeline"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    smooth-scroll: true
    self-contained: true
    css: styles.css
---

```{=html}
<div class="hero-banner">
  <h1>UOM Suggestion Logic in the<br>Recognition Pipeline</h1>
  <hr class="hero-divider">
  <p class="hero-subtitle">How the engine decides when to flag a row for analyst review</p>
</div>

<div class="meta-row">
  <div class="meta-item">
    <span class="meta-label">Author</span>
    <span class="meta-value">Omkar Malpure</span>
  </div>
  <div class="meta-item">
    <span class="meta-label">Published</span>
    <span class="meta-value">May 27, 2026</span>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="curvo-logo-block">
  <img src="curvo.png" alt="Curvo" class="curvo-logo-img"/>
</div>
```

## The Big Picture — Why Suggestions Exist

The engine's job is to figure out what UOM (unit of measure) a purchase line is in. For example, was this box of gloves bought as a **case of 200** or a **box of 100**?

Most of the time (Steps 1–4) it can figure this out exactly from price history. But in **Step 5 (DBSCAN)**, it's doing statistical guessing — it looks at what price ranges this product has been sold at in other UOMs and asks:

> *"Which UOM does this price fit into?"*

The problem is: sometimes the price fits into **two UOMs equally well**. In that case, the engine cannot confidently pick one — so instead of guessing wrong, it **flags it for a human** to review.

That flagged row is what becomes a **suggestion**.

## The Scoring System

Every candidate UOM gets a **proximity score** between 0 and 1:

| Score | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| **1.0** | Price fits perfectly into this UOM's known price range |
| **0.5** | Price is somewhat close to this UOM's range |
| **0.0** | Price has nothing to do with this UOM |

The engine picks the **highest scorer as the winner**. But then it asks:

> *Is anyone else close enough that I shouldn't be confident?*

## The Competition Rule

**Competition threshold = winner's score ÷ 1.5**

If any other candidate scores **above this threshold**, the engine considers it too close to call.

```
Winner score = 0.82
Competition threshold = 0.82 / 1.5 = 0.55

Any other candidate ≥ 0.55?
  YES → too close, flag for human review  (suggestion)
  NO  → safe to auto-assign              (recognized)
```

::: {.callout-note}
**Think of it like this:** if the winner is at 82% confidence and the runner-up is at 79%, the engine says *"I'm not sure enough — let a human decide."*
:::

## The FileUOM Boost Rule

Separately, there is a second trigger. The raw purchase file sometimes has a UOM column — like the supplier wrote `"CS"` in the file. The engine calls this the **FileUOM**.

Even if the engine's scoring picked a clear winner, if the **FileUOM points to a different UOM** that also has a reasonable score (≥ 0.15), the engine says:

> *"The file itself is labeling this differently from what I calculated. That's suspicious. A human should look at this."*

It gives the FileUOM candidate a **1.5× score boost** and **always forces a suggestion**, even if there would have been no competition otherwise.

The FileUOM candidate becomes the **top suggestion** shown first to the analyst, because the engine wants to highlight:

> *"The file said this — do you agree?"*

### The 0.30 Minimum Gate

The FileUOM boost only triggers if the FileUOM candidate's raw score is **≥ 0.30** — the same threshold used to consider any UOM "recognized" at all.

```
FILE_UOM_MIN_SCORE = 0.30
```

If FileUOM scores below 0.30, the engine ignores the file label entirely — because a UOM that scores below the recognition floor has no statistical support and is likely noise or a garbage label from the supplier.

| FileUOM raw score | Result |
|-------------------|--------|
| < 0.30 | Ignored — no statistical support, label treated as noise |
| ≥ 0.30 | Boosted by 1.5× → forced suggestion for analyst review |

### Why 1.5× — The Mathematical Symmetry

The 1.5 multiplier is not arbitrary — it mirrors the competition threshold divisor exactly:

```
Competition window  = winner ÷ 1.5  → candidates within 33% of winner
FileUOM boost       = raw score × 1.5  → 50% increase

33% = 50% ÷ 1.5   (same constant, two sides of the same coin)
```

A FileUOM boosted by 50% is designed to cross exactly into the competition window — the boost is the precise inverse of the competition divisor. If the file label scores anywhere near the competition boundary, the 1.5× lift guarantees it surfaces as the top suggestion.

::: {.callout-note}
These thresholds (0.30 minimum, 1.5× boost, 1.5 competition divisor) are **tunable hyperparameters**, not mathematically proven optima. They were chosen empirically as a reasonable starting point and should be revisited based on analyst acceptance/rejection rates over time.
:::

## What Happens to the Row

In both cases — competition or FileUOM conflict — the result is the same:

| What | Value |
|------|-------|
| `RecognizedUnitOfMeasureID` in DataLineEnriched | `NULL` — nothing auto-assigned |
| All competing candidates | Inserted into `UOMSuggestion` table |
| Row in UI | Shows in **Suggestions** tab |
| Top suggestion shown | Highest confidence candidate |

- **Analyst accepts** → suggestion deleted, value written to `DataLineEnriched` → row moves to Recognized tab
- **Analyst rejects** → suggestion marked `IsRejected = 1` → next best candidate surfaces automatically

## Example 1 — Competition

**Two UOMs both fit the price equally well.**

**Line:** Surgical gloves, PriceUOM = `$48.00`

| UOM | Qty per UOM | Known price range | Score |
|-----|------------|-------------------|-------|
| CS  | 200        | $40 – $55         | **0.82** |
| BX  | 100        | $44 – $52         | 0.79 |
| EA  | 1          | $0.20 – $0.60     | 0.10 |

**Winner = CS at 0.82** → Competition threshold = `0.82 / 1.5 =` **0.55**

- BX scored `0.79` → **above 0.55 → competition exists** ✓
- EA scored `0.10` → below 0.55, not a competitor ✗

::: {.result-box}
**Result — Suggestion created:**
`isRecognized = false` · CS and BX both inserted into `UOMSuggestion` · CS is top suggestion (0.82), BX is second (0.79) · Analyst sees: *"We think CS/200 — but BX/100 is also plausible. You decide."*
:::

## Example 2 — No Competition

**Engine is confident, auto-assigns with no suggestion.**

**Line:** Paper towels, PriceUOM = `$120.00`

| UOM | Known price range | Score |
|-----|-------------------|-------|
| CS  | $110 – $130       | **0.91** |
| EA  | $1.00 – $3.00     | 0.05 |

**Winner = CS at 0.91** → Competition threshold = `0.91 / 1.5 =` **0.61**

- EA scored `0.05` → **below 0.61 → no competition** ✗

::: {.result-box}
**Result — Auto-recognized:**
`isRecognized = true` · `RecognizedUnitOfMeasureID = CS` written directly to `DataLineEnriched` · No suggestion created — row appears in Recognized tab · Analyst doesn't need to do anything.
:::

## Example 3 — FileUOM Boost

**File label disagrees with the engine's top scorer.**

**Line:** IV bags, PriceUOM = `$95.00`, FileUOM column = **`"BX"`**

| UOM | Known price range | Score |
|-----|-------------------|-------|
| CS  | $85 – $105        | **0.88** |
| BX  | $88 – $100        | 0.40 |

**Winner = CS at 0.88** → Competition threshold = `0.88 / 1.5 =` **0.59**

BX scored `0.40` → below 0.59 → **normally no competition**. Without FileUOM, CS would be auto-assigned.

**BUT** — FileUOM = `"BX"`, and BX is a known candidate with score `0.40 ≥ 0.15`. BX gets boosted: `0.40 × 1.5 = 0.60`.

::: {.result-box}
**Result — Forced suggestion via FileUOM boost:**
`fileUomBoosted = true` → forced to suggestion · BX becomes **top suggestion**: *"FileUOM 'BX' found in candidate list. Raw score 0.40 boosted to 0.60. Auto-winner was CS — sent for analyst review."* · CS is second suggestion · Analyst sees: *"File says BX, but our scoring says CS. Which is correct?"*
:::

## Summary

| Situation | What the engine does |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Steps 1–4 match | Auto-recognizes, no suggestion |
| Step 5: clear winner, no close runners-up | Auto-recognizes, no suggestion |
| Step 5: runner-up within 33% of winner | **Suggestion — analyst picks** |
| Step 5: FileUOM disagrees with top scorer | **Suggestion — analyst picks** |
| Score below 0.30 | Nothing — row stays unrecognized |
